segments.text: Comfort ye, comfort ye my people
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Speak comfortably to Jerusalem, and cry unto her, that her warfare is accomplished, that her iniquity is pardoned: for she hath received of the LORD's hand double for all her sins.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the LORD, make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and hill shall be made low: and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough places plain:
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: And the glory of the LORD shall be revealed, and all flesh shall see it together: for the mouth of the LORD hath spoken it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The voice said,
Cry.
segments.speaker: Voice

segments.text: And he said,
What shall I cry? All flesh is grass, and all the goodliness thereof is as the flower of the field:
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: because the spirit of the LORD bloweth upon it: surely the people is grass.
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: but the word of our God shall stand for ever.
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: O Zion, that bringest good tidings, get thee up into the high mountain; O Jerusalem, that bringest good tidings, lift up thy voice with strength; lift it up, be not afraid; say unto the cities of Judah, Behold your God!
segments.speaker: Prophet

footnotes: O Zion…: or, O thou that tellest good tidings to Zion
O Jerusalem…: or, O thou that tellest good tidings to Jerusalem
heading: Good Tidings

segments.text: Behold, the Lord GOD will come with strong hand, and his arm shall rule for him: behold, his reward is with him, and his work before him.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: with strong…: or, against the strong
his work: or, recompence for his work

segments.text: He shall feed his flock like a shepherd: he shall gather the lambs with his arm, and carry them in his bosom, and shall gently lead those that are with young.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who hath measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, and meted out heaven with the span, and comprehended the dust of the earth in a measure, and weighed the mountains in scales, and the hills in a balance?
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: Who hath directed the Spirit of the LORD, or being his counsellor hath taught him?
segments.speaker: Prophet

footnotes: his…: Heb. man of his counsel

segments.text: With whom took he counsel, and who instructed him, and taught him in the path of judgment, and taught him knowledge, and shewed to him the way of understanding?
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: Behold, the nations are as a drop of a bucket, and are counted as the small dust of the balance: behold, he taketh up the isles as a very little thing.
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: And Lebanon is not sufficient to burn, nor the beasts thereof sufficient for a burnt offering.
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: All nations before him are as nothing; and they are counted to him less than nothing, and vanity.
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: To whom then will ye liken God? or what likeness will ye compare unto him?
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: To whom then will ye liken me, or shall I be equal?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Why sayest thou, O Jacob, and speakest, O Israel, My way is hid from the LORD, and my judgment is passed over from my God?
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: Hast thou not known? hast thou not heard, that the everlasting God, the LORD, the Creator of the ends of the earth, fainteth not, neither is weary? there is no searching of his understanding.
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: But they that wait upon the LORD shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles; they shall run, and not be weary; and they shall walk, and not faint.
segments.speaker: Prophet
